name: Advance America
description_short: Advance America is located at 5011 Lapalco Blvd #103, Marrero, LA 70072. Google reviewers rate the branch 5.0 stars across 1073 reviews. Call +1 504-340-0136.
description_long:
Advance America is a nationally recognized lender specializing in short-term emergency cash solutions. The Jacksonville, FL location at 1020–14 N. Edgewood Ave. is part of a multi-state network serving customers who need quick access to funds between paychecks or during financial hardships. The company has been operating for decades and maintains accreditation status, positioning itself as an established player in the emergency lending space. They explicitly serve customers seeking fast cash solutions outside traditional banking channels.

Advance America offers three primary loan products: Payday Loans (2–4 week terms, $100–$500), Installment Loans ($100–$1,000 with extended repayment over multiple payments), and Title Loans ($2,000–$25,000 using vehicle equity as collateral). All three products are available online and at physical locations. The Jacksonville store operates extended hours (9 a.m.–6 p.m. Monday–Friday, 9 a.m.–4 p.m. Saturday, closed Sunday), with bilingual Spanish-language support. Customers can pre-qualify by phone, apply online, or visit in-person. Application requirements include government-issued ID, proof of income, checking account, and Social Security Number or ITIN.

The company differentiates itself through convenience, accessibility, and product variety. Their 4.9-out-of-5 rating (125,456 reviews) on Google suggests strong customer satisfaction relative to industry norms. They offer same-day personal loans up to $1,000 (via installment loans), longer repayment terms than traditional payday lenders, and optional Western Union money transfer services at their locations. The bilingual staff and extended store hours cater to working families and Spanish-speaking communities who may struggle to access traditional bank credit.

Honestly assessed, Advance America operates in the high-cost lending sector. While convenient and fast, these products typically carry significantly higher interest rates and fees than personal banks or credit unions. Payday and title loans should be viewed as emergency-only solutions, not recurring debt sources. Customers should compare APR costs carefully and explore credit union PALs or employer advances first. The installment loan option offers longer terms than payday loans, potentially reducing the total cost burden, but still represents expensive credit relative to traditional lending.
pros: Same-day funding available for payday and installment loans up to $1,000
Title loans offer substantially larger amounts ($2,000–$25,000) for vehicle-owning customers
Installment loans allow repayment over multiple payments, reducing weekly cash-flow stress versus 2-week payday terms
Extended store hours (9 a.m.–6 p.m. weekdays, 9 a.m.–4 p.m. Saturday) accommodate working schedules
Bilingual Spanish-language support and staff at physical locations
Online application option available alongside in-store and phone pre-qualification
4.9-out-of-5 customer rating based on 125,456 reviews demonstrates consistent service quality
No explicit minimum credit score requirement; accessible to borrowers with poor or no credit history
cons: Payday and title loans carry substantially higher interest rates and fees than traditional bank loans or credit union alternatives
Title loans require vehicle as collateral, creating repossession risk if payments are missed
Installment loan maximum ($1,000) remains modest; customers with larger emergency needs cannot be served
No pricing transparency on website—APR, specific fee structures, and total cost examples are not disclosed upfront
Short repayment terms (2–4 weeks for payday loans) create high default risk and debt-trap potential for repeat borrowers
best_for: Workers with immediate cash shortfalls between paychecks who need $100–$500 within days
Vehicle owners with emergency expenses who can access larger loans ($2,000–$25,000) via title equity
Self-employed or gig workers who lack traditional employment verification but have irregular income and checking accounts
Bilingual Spanish-speaking customers in Jacksonville seeking accessible, culturally responsive lending with extended hours
diagnosis: Advance America is best for working adults facing short-term cash emergencies ($100–$1,000) or vehicle owners needing larger amounts who cannot access traditional bank credit quickly. The primary caveat: these are high-cost loans suitable only for true emergencies; borrowers should compare APR costs against credit union PALs, employer advances, or personal loans before committing, and must avoid using payday/title loans repeatedly, as the cost structure creates debt-trap risks.
services: Payday loans ($100–$500, 2–4 week terms, online or in-store)
Installment loans ($100–$1,000, multiple payment terms, online or in-store)
Title loans ($2,000–$25,000, vehicle collateral-based, online or in-store)
In-store loan processing with trained staff
Extended retail hours (9 a.m.–6 p.m. weekdays, 9 a.m.–4 p.m. Saturday)

cfpb_data.cfpb_company_name: Advance America, Cash Advance Centers, Inc.
